title: calamagrostis hyperborea var. americana
intro: Calamagrostis hyperborea var. americana, also known as American wood grass or Borealo-American meadow grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. Its health benefits include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. Calamagrostis hyperborea var. americana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness.
